edges.description: The aberrantly activated P250R receptor sustains downstream MAPK/ERK signaling in cranial suture osteoblasts.
edges.target: Sustained MAPK/ERK signaling in suture osteoblasts

edges.description: Sustained MAPK/ERK signaling accelerates osteogenic differentiation and premature suture fusion.
edges.source: Sustained MAPK/ERK signaling in suture osteoblasts

edges.description: Premature coronal suture fusion manifests clinically as coronal craniosynostosis.

nodes.description: Uni- or bilateral coronal suture fusion is the most common feature. Bilateral involvement causes brachycephaly; unilateral causes plagiocephaly.

evidence.reference_title: Muenke syndrome: An international multicenter natural history study.
evidence.snippet: Eighty-five percent of the participants had craniosynostosis (16 of 103 did not have craniosynostosis)

evidence.reference_title: A unique point mutation in the fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 gene (FGFR3) defines a new craniosynostosis syndrome.
evidence.snippet: coronal synostosis is due to an amino acid substitution (Pro250Arg) that results from a single point mutation in the fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 gene

nodes.description: The P250R mutation in FGFR3 causes altered ligand binding specificity, allowing aberrant activation by non-cognate FGF ligands. In cranial sutures, this leads to premature osteogenic differentiation and suture fusion. The mechanism differs from achondroplasia-type mutations that affect growth plate chondrocytes.

evidence.reference_title: The molecular and genetic basis of fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 disorders: the achondroplasia family of skeletal dysplasias, Muenke craniosynostosis, and Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: Recent evidence suggests that the phenotypic differences may be due to specific alleles with varying degrees of ligand-independent activation, allowing the receptor to be constitutively active.

nodes.description: Premature osteogenic differentiation in the coronal suture causes early coronal suture fusion, the predominant craniosynostosis pattern of Muenke syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: A unique point mutation in the fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 gene (FGFR3) defines a new craniosynostosis syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Here we present 61 individuals from 20 unrelated families where coronal synostosis is due to an amino acid substitution (Pro250Arg) that results from a single point mutation in the fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 gene on chromosome 4p.

nodes.description: The P250R FGFR3 receptor, with its altered ligand specificity, drives sustained MAPK/ERK signaling in osteoblast-lineage cells of the cranial suture. This is the conserved effector branch shared with the other FGFR craniosynostoses; in FGFR3 the receptor shows accelerated, predominantly ligand-dependent ERK activation.
nodes.id: Sustained MAPK/ERK signaling in suture osteoblasts

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 mutations promote apoptosis but do not alter chondrocyte proliferation in thanatophoric dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Early ERKs activation following FGF 9 supplementation was observed in TD chondrocytes (2 min) as compared with controls (5 min) but no signal was detected in the absence of ligand.
